sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presented with neck pain after being involved in a car accident one week prior, where he was hit in the back and his neck was thrown forward.
He reports pain severity as a 7 out of 10, which is exacerbated by movement of his neck.
The pain is localized to the back of his neck and has not improved significantly with ibuprofen.
He has had occasional headaches, but no hearing or visual disturbances.
He has a history of playing football, but no previous neck injuries.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, pain with flexion, extension, and rotation of the neck, and lateral bending.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain causing his neck pain.
Problem list: Neck strain; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6 to 8 hours for inflammation.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to be conservative with his neck movements and avoid straining.
I ordered an MRI to rule out a concussion or nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further treatment.
Follow-up: I will review the results of the MRI and schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss further treatment.
